question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2023

Code: 65/2/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and follow them:

  1. This Question Paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. Question paper is divided into FIVE Sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A - Question Nos. 1 to 18 are Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and Question Nos. 19 & 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B - Question Nos. 21 to 25 are Very Short Answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C - Question Nos. 26 to 31 are Short Answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D - Question Nos. 32 to 35 are Long Answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E - Question Nos. 36 to 38 are source based/case based/passage based/integrated units of assessment questions carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 3 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is NOT allowed.

SECTION - A
(Multiple Choice Questions)
Each question carries 1 mark.
1.

If the angle between {tex}\vec{a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{\pi}{3}{/tex} and {tex}|\vec{a} \times \vec{b}|{/tex} = {tex}3 \sqrt{3}{/tex}, then the value of {tex}\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}{/tex} is

a. 9
b. 3
c. {tex}\frac 19{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
1
2.

The position vectors of three consecutive vertices of a parallelogram ABCD are A{tex}(4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}}-6 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}, B{tex}(5 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+\hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex} and C{tex}(12 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+4 \hat{\mathrm{j}}+5 \hat{\mathrm{k}}){/tex}. The position vector of D is given by

a. {tex}-3 \hat{\text i}-5 \hat{\text j}-10 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
b. {tex}21 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}{/tex}
c. {tex}11 \hat{\text i}+9 \hat{\text j}-2 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
d. {tex}-11 \hat{\text i}-9 \hat{\text j}+2 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
1
3.

If for two events A and B, P(A - B) = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} and P(A) = {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}, then {tex}\text P\left(\frac{\text B}{\text A}\right){/tex} is equal to

a. {tex}\frac 12{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac 35{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac 25{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 23{/tex}
1
4.

If {tex}\int\limits_0^{2 \pi}{/tex} cos2x dx = k {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac \pi 2}{/tex} cos2x dx, then the value of k is

a. 4
b. 2
c. 1
d. 0
1
5.

If (a, b), (c, d) and (e, f) are the vertices of {tex}\triangle \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} and {tex}\triangle{/tex} denotes the area of {tex}\triangle \mathrm{ABC}{/tex}, then {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll}\mathrm{a} & \mathrm{c} & \mathrm{e} \\ \mathrm{b} & \mathrm{d} & \mathrm{f} \\ 1 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right|^2{/tex} is equal to

a. {tex}2 \triangle^2{/tex}
b. {tex}4\triangle^2{/tex}
c. {tex}2 \triangle{/tex}
d. {tex}4\triangle{/tex}
1
6.

The function f(x) = x|x| is

a. continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
b. continuous but not differentiable at x = 0.
c. differentiable but not continuous at x = 0.
d. neither differentiable nor continuous at x = 0.
1
7.

If tan {tex}\left(\frac{\text x+\mathrm{y}}{\text x-\mathrm{y}}\right){/tex} = k, then {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} \text x}{/tex} is equal to

a. {tex}\frac {-\text y}{\text x}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac {\text y}{\text x}{/tex}
c. sec2{tex}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex}
d. -sec2{tex}\left(\frac{\mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{x}}\right){/tex}
1
8.

The objective function Z = ax + by of an LPP has maximum value 42 at (4, 6) and minimum value 19 at (3, 2). Which of the following is true?

a. a = 9, b = 1
b. a = 5, b = 2
c. a = 3, b = 5
d. a = 5, b = 3
1
9.

The corner points of the feasible region of a linear programming problem are (0, 4), (8, 0) and {tex}\left(\frac{20}{3}, \frac {4}{3}\right){/tex}. If Z = 30x + 24y is the objective function, then (maximum value of Z - minimum value of Z) is equal to

a. 144
b. 96
c. 120
d. 136
1
10.

Number of symmetric matrices of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 with each entry 1 or -1 is

a. 512
b. 64
c. 8
d. 4
1
11.

If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 0 \\ 5 & 4\end{array}\right]{/tex} = P + Q, where P is a symmetric and Q is a skew symmetric matrix, then Q is equal to

a. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & \frac 52 \\ \frac 5 2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
b. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -\frac 52 \\ \frac 5 2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
c. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & \frac 52 \\ -\frac 5 2 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
d. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 2 & -\frac 52 \\ \frac 52 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
1
12.

If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 1 \\ 3 & \mathrm{a} & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} is non-singular matrix and a {tex}\in {/tex} A, then the set A is

a. {tex}\mathbb R{/tex}
b. {0}
c. {4}
d. {tex}\mathbb R{/tex} - {4}
1
13.

If |A| = |kA|, where A is a square matrix of order 2, then sum of all possible values of k is

a. 1
b. -1
c. 2
d. 0
1
14.

If {tex}\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{d} x}{/tex}[f(x)] = ax + b and f(0) = 0, then f(x) is equal to

a. a + b
b. {tex}\frac{\text {ax}^2}{2}+\text {bx}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\text {ax}^2}{2}+\text {bx}{/tex} + c
d. b
1
15.

Degree of the differential equation sin x + cos{tex}\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right){/tex} = y2 is

a. 2
b. 1
c. not defined
d. 0
1
16.

The integrating factor of the differential equation (1 - y2){tex}\frac{\text {dx}}{\text {dy}}{/tex} + yx = ay, (-1 < y < 1) is

a. {tex}\frac{1}{\text y^2-1}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt {\text y^2-1}}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{1}{1-\text y^2}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt {1-\text y^2}}{/tex}
1
17.

Unit vector along {tex}\overrightarrow{\text {PQ}}{/tex}, where coordinates of P and Q respectively are (2, 1, -1) and (4, 4, -7), is

a. {tex}2 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}-6 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
b. {tex}-2 \hat{\text i}-3 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{-2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}}{7}-\frac{3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}}{7}+\frac{6 \hat{\mathrm{k}}}{7}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{2 \hat{\mathrm{i}}}{7}+\frac{3 \hat{\mathrm{j}}}{7}-\frac{6 \hat{\mathrm{k}}}{7}{/tex}
1
18.

Equation of a line passing through point (1, 2, 3) and equally inclined to the coordinate axis, is

a. {tex}\frac{\text x}{1}=\frac{\text y}{2}=\frac{\text z}{3}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{\text x}{1}=\frac{\text y}{1}=\frac{\text z}{1}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{\text x-1}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-1}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-1}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{\text x-1}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-2}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{z}-3}{1}{/tex}
1

ASSERTION-REASON BASED QUESTIONS

In the following questions 19 & 20, a statement of Assertion (A) is followed by a statement of Reason (R).
19.

Assertion (A): Maximum value of (cos-1 x) 2 is {tex}\pi^2{/tex}.
Reason (R): Range of the principal value branch of cos-1x is {tex}\left[\frac{-\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]{/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
20.

Assertion (A): If a line makes angles {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\gamma{/tex} with positive direction of the coordinate axes, then sin2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2.
Reason (R): The sum of squares of the direction cosines of a line is 1.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

SECTION – B

This section comprises of Very Short Answer Type (VSA) questions, each of 2 marks.
21.

If points A, B and C have position vectors {tex}2 \hat{\text i}, \hat{\text j}{/tex} and {tex}2 \hat{\text k}{/tex} respectively, then show that {tex}\triangle \mathrm{ABC}{/tex} is an isosceles triangle.

 2
22.(i)

Evaluate sin-1 {tex}\left(\sin \frac{3 \pi}{4}\right){/tex} + cos-1(cos {tex}\pi{/tex}) + tan-1(1).

 2

OR
(ii)

Draw the graph of cos-1 x, where x {tex}\in{/tex}[-1, 0]. Also, write its range.

 2
23.

If equal sides of an isosceles triangle with fixed base 10 cm are increasing at the rate of 4 cm/sec, how fast is the area of triangle increasing at an instant when all sides become equal?

 2
24.

Find the coordinates of points on line {tex}\frac{x}{1}=\frac{y-1}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{z+1}{2}{/tex} which are at a distance of {tex}\sqrt{11}{/tex} units from origin.

 2
25.(i)

If y = {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{a} x+\mathrm{b}}{/tex}, prove that {tex}\mathrm{y}\left(\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{d} x^2}\right)+\left(\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{d} x}\right)^2{/tex} = 0.

 2

OR
(ii)

If f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{lll} \mathrm{a} x+\mathrm{b} & ; & 0<x \leq 1 \\ 2 x^2-x & ; & 1<x<2 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is a differentiable function in (0, 2), then find the values of a and b.

 2

SECTION – C

The section comprises Short Answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.

Solve the following Linear Programming problem graphically:
Maximize: Z = 3x + 3.5y
subject to constraints: x + 2y {tex}\geq{/tex} 240,
3x + 1.5y {tex}\geq{/tex} 270,
1.5x + 2y {tex}\leq{/tex} 310,
x {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0.

 3
27.(i)

Find {tex}\int \frac{\text x+2}{\sqrt{\text x^2-4 \text x-5}} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}.

 3

OR
(ii)

Evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{-\mathrm{a}}^{\mathrm{a}}{/tex}f(x) dx, where f(x) = {tex}\frac{9^\text x}{1+9^\text x}{/tex}.

 3
28.

Find {tex}\int \text e^{\cot ^{-1} \text x}\left(\frac{1-\text x+\text x^2}{1+\text x^2}\right) \mathrm{dx}{/tex}.

 3
29.

Evaluate {tex}\int\limits_{\log \sqrt{2}}^{\log \sqrt{3}} \frac{1}{\left(\mathrm{e}^\text x+\mathrm{e}^{-\text x}\right)\left(\mathrm{e}^\text x-\mathrm{e}^{-\text x}\right)} \mathrm{dx}{/tex}

 3
30.(i)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (xy - x2) dy = y2 dx.

 3

OR
(ii)

Find the general solution of the differential equation:
(x2 + 1) {tex}\frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}{/tex} + 2xy = {tex}\sqrt{\text x^2+4}{/tex}

 3
31.(i)

Two numbers are selected from first six even natural numbers at random without replacement. If X denotes the greater of two numbers selected, find the probability distribution of X.

 3

OR
(ii)

A fair coin and an unbiased die are tossed. Let A be the event, Head appears on the coin and B be the event, 3 comes on the die. Find whether A and B are independent events or not.

 3

SECTION – D

This section comprises Long Answer type (LA) questions of 5 marks each.
32.

A function f: [-4, 4] {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} [0, 4] is given by f(x) = {tex}\sqrt{16-x^2}{/tex}. Show that f is an onto function but not a one-one function. Further, find all possible values of a for which f(a) = {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}.

 5
33.(i)

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find AB and use it to solve the following system of equations:
x - 2y = 3
2x - y - z = 2
-2y + z = 3

 5

OR
(ii)

If f({tex}\alpha{/tex}) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} \cos \alpha & -\sin \alpha & 0 \\ \sin \alpha & \cos \alpha & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, prove that f({tex}\alpha{/tex}){tex} \cdot {/tex}f({tex}-\beta{/tex}) = f({tex}\alpha{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex})

 5
34.(i)

Find the equations of the diagonals of the parallelogram PQRS whose vertices are P(4, 2, -6), Q(5, -3, 1), R(12, 4, 5) and S(11, 9, -2). Use these equations to find the point of intersection of diagonals.

 5

OR
(ii)

A line l passes through point (-1, 3, -2) and is perpendicular to both the lines {tex}\frac{x}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x+2}{-3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{y}-1}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{z}+1}{5}{/tex}. Find the vector equation of the line l. Hence, obtain its distance from origin.

 5
35.

Find the area of the smaller region bounded by the curves {tex}\frac{x^2}{25}+\frac{y^2}{16}{/tex} = 1 and {tex}\frac{x}{5}+\frac{y}{4}{/tex} = 1, using integration.

 5

SECTION – E

This section comprises 3 source based case-based/passage based/integrated units of assessment questions of 4 marks each.
36.

Recent studies suggest that roughly 12% of the world population is left handed.

Depending upon the parents, the chances of having a left handed child are as follows:

  1. When both father and mother are left handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 24%.
  2. When father is right handed and mother is left handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 22%.
  3. When father is left handed and mother is right handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 17%.
  4. When both father and mother are right handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 9%.

Assuming that P(A) = P(B) = P(C) = P(D) = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} and L denotes the event that child is left handed.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text L}{\text C}\right){/tex}

 1
(ii)

Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\bar {\text L}}{\text A}\right){/tex}

 1
(iii)(a)

Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text A}{\text L}\right){/tex}

 2

OR
(b)

Find the probability that a randomly selected child is left handed given that exactly one of the parents is left handed.

 2
37.

Engine displacement is the measure of the cylinder volume swept by all the pistons of a piston engine. The piston moves inside the cylinder bore

The cylinder bore in the form of circular cylinder open at the top is to be made from a metal sheet of area {tex}75\pi{/tex} cm2.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

If the radius of cylinder is r cm and height is h cm, then write the volume V of cylinder in terms of radius r.

 1
(ii)

Find {tex}\frac {\text {dV}}{\text {dr}}{/tex}.

 1
(iii)(a)

Find the radius of cylinder when its volume is maximum.

 2

OR
(b)

For maximum volume, h > r. State true or false and justify.

 2
38.

The use of electric vehicles will curb air pollution in the long run.

The use of electric vehicles is increasing every year and estimated electric vehicles in use at any time t is given by the function V:
V(t) = {tex}\frac{1}{5} \text t^3-\frac{5}{2} \text t^2{/tex} + 25t - 2
where t represents the time and t = 1, 2, 3... corresponds to year 2001, 2002, 2003, ... respectively.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Can the above function be used to estimate number of vehicles in the year 2000? Justify.

 2
(ii)

Prove that the function V(t) is an increasing function.

 2

The function f(x) = x|x| is

a. continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
b. continuous but not differentiable at x = 0.
c. differentiable but not continuous at x = 0.
d. neither differentiable nor continuous at x = 0.
Correct Answer: (a) continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
Explanation:

continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
Given f(x) = x|x|
function can be written as,
{tex} f(x) = \begin{cases} x(x) & \quad \text{if } x\geq0\\ x(-x) & \quad \text{if } x<0 \end{cases}{/tex}
{tex} f(x) = \begin{cases} x^2 & \quad \text{if } x\geq0\\ -x^2 & \quad \text{if } x<0 \end{cases}{/tex}
Now, 

{tex}Rf(0)=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to 0^-} (-x^2)\end{equation}{/tex} = 0

{tex}Lf(0)=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to 0^+} (x^2)\end{equation}{/tex} = 0

f(0) = 0

{tex}\because Rf(0) = Lf(0) = f(0){/tex}
so f(x) is continuous at x=0
Now
{tex}Rf'(0)=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to o^-} \tfrac{f(x)-f(0)}{x-0} \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to o^-} \tfrac{(-x^2)-0}{x-0} \end{equation}=0{/tex}

{tex}Lf'(0)=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to o^+} \tfrac{f(x)-f(0)}{x-0} \end{equation}{/tex}

{tex}=\begin{equation} \lim_{x\to o^+} \tfrac{x^2-0}{x-0} \end{equation}=0{/tex}

{tex}\because Rf'(0)=Lf'(x){/tex}
so f(x) is continuous at x=0
hence f(x) is continuous and differentiable at x = 0.

Explanation:

{tex}\frac {\text y}{\text x}{/tex}
tan {tex}\left(\frac{\text x+\mathrm{y}}{\text x-\mathrm{y}}\right){/tex} = k

{tex}\left(\frac{\text x+\mathrm{y}}{\text x-\mathrm{y}}\right){/tex} = tan-1k

{tex}\frac{d}{dx}\left(\frac{\text x+\mathrm{y}}{\text x-\mathrm{y}}\right){/tex}= {tex}\frac {d}{dx}tan^{-1}x{/tex}


{tex}\frac{(x-y)\frac{d}{dx}(\text x+\mathrm{y})-(x+y)\frac{d}{dx}{(x-y)}}{(\text x-\mathrm{y})^2} =0{/tex}

{tex}(x-y)(1+\frac{dy}{dx}) - (x+y)(1-\frac{dy}{dx})=0{/tex}

{tex}(x-y)(1+\frac{dy}{dx}) = (x+y)(1-\frac{dy}{dx}){/tex}

{tex}(x-y)+(x-y)\frac{dy}{dx} = (x+y)-(x-y)\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}

{tex}[(x-y)+(x-y)]\frac{dy}{dx} = x+y-x+y{/tex}
2x {tex}\frac{dy}{dx}{/tex}= 2y
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y}{x}{/tex}

Reason (R): The sum of squares of the direction cosines of a line is 1.

b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 - (sin2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\gamma{/tex}) = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2

If f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{lll} \mathrm{a} x+\mathrm{b} & ; & 0<x \leq 1 \\ 2 x^2-x & ; & 1<x<2 \end{array}\right.{/tex} is a differentiable function in (0, 2), then find the values of a and b.

Solution:

f(x) = {tex}\left\{\begin{array}{lll} \mathrm{a} x+\mathrm{b} & ; & 0<x \leq 1 \\ 2 x^2-x & ; & 1<x<2 \end{array}\right.{/tex}

LHD at x=1
={tex}\lim\limits_{\text x \rightarrow 1^{-}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{f(x)-f(1)}{x-1}{/tex}

= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{f(1-h)-f(1)}{1-h-1}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[a(1-h)+b]-[a+b]}{-h}{/tex}

= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[a-ah+b-a-b]}{-h}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[-ah]}{-h}{/tex}
= a
RHD at x=1

={tex}\lim\limits_{\text x \rightarrow 1^{+}}{/tex} {tex}\frac{f(x)-f(1)}{x-1}{/tex}

= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{f(1+h)-f(1)}{1+h-1}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[2(1+h)^2+(1+h)]-[2-1]}{h}{/tex}

= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[2(1+h^2+2h)-1-h]-1}{h}{/tex}
={tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[2+2h^2+4h-1-h-1]}{h}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}\frac{[2h^2+3h]}{h}{/tex}

={tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}{[2h+3]}{/tex}
=3

Since f(x) is differentiable, so LHD=RHD
a=3
Now LHL
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text x \rightarrow 1^{-}}f(x){/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}{f(1-h)}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}{a(1-h)+b}{/tex}

= a+b
Now, RHL
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text x \rightarrow 1^{-}}f(x){/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}{f(1+h)}{/tex}
= {tex}\lim\limits_{\text h \rightarrow 0}{2(1+h)^2-(1+h)}{/tex}
=2-1=1
f(x) is differentiable in (0, 2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) is continuous on (0, 2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} f(x) is continuous at x = 1
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + b = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} b = 1-a
b=1-3
b=-2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} a = 3 & b = -2

Find the general solution of the differential equation: (xy - x2) dy = y2 dx.

Solution:

Given differential equation can be written as
{tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text y^2}{\text {xy}-\text x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\left(\frac{\text y}{\text x}\right)^2}{\frac{\text y}{\text x}-1}{/tex}
Put y = ux, {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} u + {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u^2}{\text u-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\text x\frac{\text {du}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text u}{\text u-1}{/tex}
Separating the variables and integrating
{tex}\int\left(1-\frac{1}{\text u}\right) \text {du}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{\text {dx}}{\text x}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u - log u = log |x| + c {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\text y}{\text x}-\log \frac{\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = log |x| + c

A function f: [-4, 4] {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} [0, 4] is given by f(x) = {tex}\sqrt{16-x^2}{/tex}. Show that f is an onto function but not a one-one function. Further, find all possible values of a for which f(a) = {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}.

Solution:

A function f: [-4, 4] {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} [0, 4] is given by f(x) = {tex}\sqrt{16-x^2}{/tex}
One-One: f(-1) = f(1) = {tex}\sqrt{15}{/tex} but -1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 1,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} f(x) is not a one-one function.
Onto: Let y = {tex}\sqrt{16-x^2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0
Squaring we get,
y2 = 16 - x2
x2 = 16 - y2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\pm \sqrt{16-\text y^2}{/tex}
For each y {tex}\in{/tex}[-4, 4], 'x' is a real number,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 0 {tex}\leq{/tex} y {tex}\leq{/tex} 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Rf = [0, 4] = Co-domain
{tex}\therefore{/tex} f(x) is an onto function.
f(a) = {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{16-a^2}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{7}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 16 - a2 = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = {tex}\pm 3{/tex}

Recent studies suggest that roughly 12% of the world population is left handed.

Depending upon the parents, the chances of having a left handed child are as follows:

  1. When both father and mother are left handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 24%.
  2. When father is right handed and mother is left handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 22%.
  3. When father is left handed and mother is right handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 17%.
  4. When both father and mother are right handed:
    Chances of left handed child is 9%.

Assuming that P(A) = P(B) = P(C) = P(D) = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} and L denotes the event that child is left handed.

  1. Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text L}{\text C}\right){/tex}. (1)
  2. Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\bar {\text L}}{\text A}\right){/tex}. (1)
  3. Find {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text A}{\text L}\right){/tex}. (2)
    OR
    Find the probability that a randomly selected child is left handed given that exactly one of the parents is left handed. (2)
Solution:
  1. {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text L}{\text C}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac {17}{100}{/tex}
  2. {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\bar {\text L}}{\text A}\right){/tex} = 1 - {tex}\text P\left(\frac { {\text L}}{\text A}\right){/tex} = 1 - {tex}\frac {24}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac {76}{100}{/tex} or {tex}\frac {19}{25}{/tex}
  3. {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text A}{\text L}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{24}{100}}{\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{24}{100}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{22}{100}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{17}{100}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{9}{100}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{24}{72}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}
    Probability that a randomly selected child is left-handed given that exactly one of the parents is left-handed.
    = {tex}\text P\left(\frac {\text L}{\text B \cup \text C}\right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{22}{100}+\frac{17}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{39}{100}{/tex}

    OR

  1. {tex}\pi r^2{/tex} + {tex}2 \pi r h{/tex} = {tex}75 \pi{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = {tex}\frac{75-r^2}{2 r}{/tex}, {tex}\therefore{/tex} V = {tex}\pi r^2 h{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}(75r - r3)
  2. {tex}\frac {\text {dV}}{\text {dr}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex}(75 - 3r2)
  3. {tex}\left.\frac{\text {dV}}{\text {dr}}=0 \Rightarrow \text r=5, \frac{\text d^2 \text V}{\text {dr}^2}\right]_{\text r=5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac \pi 2{/tex}(-6r) < 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} volume is maximum when r = 5
    OR

    {tex}\left.\frac{\text {dV}}{\text {dr}}=0 \Rightarrow \text r=5, \frac{\text d^2 \text V}{\text {dr}^2}\right]_{\mathrm{r}=5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}(-6r) < 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} volume is maximum when r = 5
    As volume is maximum at r = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = {tex}\frac{75-5^2}{2(5)}{/tex} = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = r

The use of electric vehicles will curb air pollution in the long run.

The use of electric vehicles is increasing every year and estimated electric vehicles in use at any time t is given by the function V:
V(t) = {tex}\frac{1}{5} \text t^3-\frac{5}{2} \text t^2{/tex} + 25t - 2
where t represents the time and t = 1, 2, 3... corresponds to year 2001, 2002, 2003, ... respectively.
Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Can the above function be used to estimate number of vehicles in the year 2000? Justify. (2)
  2. Prove that the function V(t) is an increasing function. (2)
Solution:
  1. For the year 2000, t = 0 & V(0) = -2 and the number of vehicles cannot be negative
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} the given function V(t) cannot be used.
  2. V'(t) = {tex}\frac{3}{5}\text t^2{/tex} - 5t + 25 = {tex}\frac{3}{5}\left[\left(t-\frac{25}{6}\right)^2+\frac{875}{36}\right]{/tex} > 0,
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} V(t) is an increasing function.
